title: Listen and Choose a Response — Daily Encounters

questions.stem: Excuse me, do you have the time? My phone battery died.

options.B: You should always carry a watch.
options.C: My phone battery lasts all day.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking for the current time, so providing it directly is the most appropriate response.

questions.stem: Oh no, I'm so sorry! I spilled my coffee on your notes.

options.A: It's okay, accidents happen. I have a digital copy anyway.
options.B: You should be more careful with hot drinks.
options.C: Coffee stains are very hard to remove.

questions.explanation: The speaker is apologizing for a small accident, so reassuring them and minimizing the impact is gracious.

questions.stem: It's been raining nonstop for three days. I'm tired of it.

options.A: I know what you mean. I wish spring weather would arrive already.

questions.explanation: The speaker is complaining about the weather, so showing agreement and shared sentiment builds rapport.

questions.stem: I lost my student ID somewhere on campus. Is there a lost and found office?

options.A: Yes, check at the campus security office in the student center.
options.B: Student IDs are required for library access.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about where to find lost items, so providing the location of the lost and found is directly helpful.

questions.stem: My advisor approved my research proposal! I can finally start collecting data.

options.A: That's wonderful news! Your proposal was very well written.
options.B: Research proposals require ethics board approval too.
options.C: Data collection can take several months.

questions.explanation: The speaker is sharing exciting academic news, so sharing their enthusiasm and complimenting their work is encouraging.

questions.stem: I'm expecting a package. Where do dorm residents usually pick up mail?

options.A: Packages are delivered to the front desk of your dorm building.
options.B: Mail delivery times vary by location.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about mail pickup procedures, so providing the specific location is helpful.

questions.stem: I can't thank you enough for taking notes for me while I was sick.

options.A: Of course! I hope you're feeling better now.
options.B: Missing class can put you behind.
options.C: I take notes for all my classes.

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing deep gratitude, so acknowledging the thanks warmly and expressing concern for their health is caring.

questions.stem: Does the campus shuttle go to the train station, or do I need to take the city bus?

options.A: The campus shuttle stops at the train station every hour on the hour.
options.B: Train stations are usually located downtown.
options.C: I prefer driving to taking public transportation.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about transportation options, so providing clear information about the shuttle service is directly useful.

Oh no, I'm so sorry! I spilled my coffee on your notes.
It's been raining nonstop for three days. I'm tired of it.
I lost my student ID somewhere on campus. Is there a lost and found office?
